ports.purpose: Discover configured authoritative artifacts and return stable paths, types, sizes, timestamps, and content hashes.
Resolve repository revision, baseline, tag, branch, dirty state, and commit provenance.
Extract requirements, attributes, hierarchy, and requirement-set provenance.
Extract typed incoming and outgoing lifecycle trace relationships.
Extract model, Stateflow, harness, interface, parameter, variant, and data-dictionary evidence.
Extract test definitions, executions, iterations, result sets, verdicts, and authoritative coverage summaries.
Read controlled XLSX/CSV plans, matrices, results, review registers, CM, QA, and certification records.
Index C/H files, functions, interfaces, generated classification, and content provenance without acting as an IDE.
Import reviews, checklists, independence, decisions, approvals, comments, and attachments.
Import authoritative model/code coverage results without calculating coverage.
Import program-owned objective identifiers, applicability, status, and satisfaction links without bundling licensed standard text.
Atomically stage and publish immutable normalized snapshots and append-only hub overlays.
Load and verify the immutable normalized sample snapshot used when no real project is configured.
Write deterministic audit-package archives without exposing storage technology to assurance workflows.

adapters.failureIsolation: A scan failure produces diagnostics and cannot replace the last valid snapshot.
Non-Git roots remain usable with explicit revision diagnostics.
Missing licenses, products, or compatible tool versions affect only MATLAB-owned sources.
Malformed workbooks are isolated per source with row/sheet diagnostics.
Unsupported syntax is reported per translation unit without blocking unrelated sources.
Review records with incomplete identity or independence are imported with explicit findings.
Coverage is never inferred; missing or unsupported reports remain visible gaps.
An explicitly configured invalid objective profile rejects the candidate snapshot; absence of a profile leaves objective evidence program-configurable.
Bundled normalized data is immutable; user changes are stored only in the resettable overlay.
Atomic rename and validation preserve the last published snapshot after interrupted writes.
A failed package write cannot mutate a published snapshot or authoritative lifecycle artifact.
